hypotheses: The FSM in TopModule is misaligned with the reference model in the timing of when out_bytes and done are asserted, causing persistent mismatches starting at time 210 [evidence: log_excerpt L9-L10 'Hint: Output ... First mismatch occurred at time 210.']

The output out_bytes is being constructed from byte registers that may not contain the correct values at the time S_DONE is entered, due to the sequence of register updates and state transitions [evidence: RTL L44 'out_bytes <= {byte1, byte2, byte3};', L45 'done <= 1'b1;']

The done signal is asserted in S_DONE, but the byte registers are only updated in previous states, potentially causing a one-cycle misalignment between when the third byte is captured and when out_bytes/done are asserted [evidence: RTL L40-L41 'byte3 <= in; state <= S_DONE;', L44-L45 'out_bytes <= {byte1, byte2, byte3}; done <= 1'b1;']

The testbench stimulus intentionally sets in[3]=1 for the first byte of each 3-byte sequence, but the FSM may not be correctly synchronized to this pattern, leading to incorrect byte grouping [evidence: TB L22-L28, stimulus_gen]

The reset logic appears to be functioning (reset toggles at 170-180, 930-940, etc.), but the FSM may not be correctly re-initializing or may be missing edge cases after reset [evidence: waveform_excerpt signal=tb.reset time=170-180, 930-940, etc.]
likely_failure_points: State transition from S_B2 to S_DONE: byte3 is captured in S_B2, but out_bytes is updated in S_DONE, possibly using stale or misaligned byte values [evidence: RTL L40-L44]

Assertion of done and out_bytes in S_DONE may be one cycle late relative to the reference model, causing persistent mismatches [evidence: repeated failure signature sim:5544da1853d8 in attempt_history, log_excerpt L9-L10]
FSM logic for capturing bytes and asserting done may not be correctly synchronized with in[3] as required by the testbench's byte grouping protocol [evidence: TB L22-L28, RTL L30-L32, L46-L48]

Reset handling: if the FSM or datapath registers are not synchronously reset or if there is a race condition at reset deassertion, outputs may be misaligned [evidence: waveform_excerpt signal=tb.reset, RTL L19-L26]
recommended_probes: Add waveform probes for TopModule.byte1, byte2, byte3, out_bytes, done, and state to observe their values at and around time 210 and during each S_DONE transition
Probe the in signal and in[3] specifically to confirm that the FSM is correctly detecting the start of a new 3-byte sequence
Add an assertion or print statement in S_DONE to log the values of byte1, byte2, byte3, out_bytes, and done at the moment they are updated
Instrument the testbench to log when in[3] is high and correlate this with the FSM state transitions in the DUT
Check the reference model's timing for done and out_bytes assertion to compare against the DUT at the first mismatch time
analysis_notes: The failure signature (sim:5544da1853d8) is identical across all three attempts, indicating that previous patch strategies (FSM restructuring, output timing adjustments) have not resolved the core issue. The persistent mismatches in both done and out_bytes, with the first mismatch at time 210, strongly suggest a systematic timing misalignment between the DUT and the reference model. The FSM currently captures byte3 in S_B2 and asserts done/out_bytes in S_DONE, but this may be one cycle off from the reference model, especially if the reference asserts done/out_bytes immediately after the third byte is captured. The testbench enforces a protocol where in[3]=1 marks the start of a 3-byte group, but the FSM may not be perfectly synchronized to this, leading to grouping errors. There is no evidence of X propagation or reset issues in the provided logs or waveforms, but further probing is needed to rule out subtle reset misalignments. To break the stagnation, the next attempt should focus on direct, cycle-accurate comparison of byte register values, state, and output signals at the first mismatch time (210) and during each S_DONE transition, as well as explicit correlation with the reference model's output timing. If the FSM is indeed one cycle late, consider updating out_bytes and done in the same cycle as byte3 is captured, or restructure the FSM to match the reference model's output protocol. Evidence is sufficient to prioritize these checks, but direct waveform comparison of all relevant signals is needed to falsify or confirm the timing misalignment hypothesis.
